Tool Usage — Run #4 (max tools overall — no verifier pass) (60 calls)

quickbooks_search_vendors1
quickbooks_search_invoices1

stripe_list_connected_accounts1

conversations_add_message1
Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Search QuickBooks for Ballantyne Appraisal Services bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria filtering by vendor "Ballantyne" or similar). Agent should retrieve all 67 bills and compute the per-appraisal average. Ballantyne has no batch summary bills all 67 are individual per-loan appraisals. Verified per-job average: approximately $472 (sum $31,637 / 67 bills = $472.19).
2. Search QuickBooks for DeLuca Appraisal Group bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria filtering by vendor "DeLuca" or similar). Agent should retrieve 75 bills total, but must notice that one bill (BILL-2026-0410-014, DocNumber DL-4107, $2,450, dated 2026-04-10) is a "March appraisal invoices batch" a rollup summary, not a per-appraisal charge. For the per-job average, exclude this batch bill. Verified per-job average: approximately $467 (74 individual bills averaging $467.41). Including the batch bill would incorrectly inflate the average to $494.
3. Search QuickBooks for Carolina Valuation Services bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria filtering by vendor "Carolina" or similar). Agent should retrieve 60 bills total, but must notice that one bill (BILL-2026-0410-015, DocNumber CVS-8831, $1,745, dated 2026-04-10) is a "March appraisal invoices batch" a rollup summary, not a per-appraisal charge. For the per-job average, exclude this batch bill. Verified per-job average: approximately $479 (59 individual bills averaging $479.02). Including the batch bill would incorrectly inflate the average to $500.
4. Search Stripe for appraisal fee charges using stripe_search_charges (query filtering by description "Appraisal fee" or similar) or by paginating through stripe_list_charges. Each charge description includes a loan number in the format "Appraisal fee -- [Surname] (LN-XXXX-XXXXX)." The agent needs these to cross-reference what borrowers were billed against what vendors charged Keystone.
5. For a sample of appraisal charges, look up the corresponding loan in the LOS using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number parsed from the Stripe charge description) to determine which appraisal vendor was used, then compare the Stripe charge amount (what the borrower paid) against the QuickBooks bill amount (what the vendor charged) to identify files where Keystone absorbed the difference. Universe data confirms approximately 56 loan files where the Stripe borrower charge is less than the QuickBooks vendor bill (e.g., LN-2024-00186 ~$75 absorbed, LN-2024-00192 ~$73 absorbed, LN-2024-00041 ~$49 absorbed). This is a multi-hop cross-reference: Stripe charge → LOS loan → QB vendor bill.
6. Search QuickBooks for overdue vendor bills using quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_show_data to find bills with Balance > 0. Agent should discover at least: (1) office supplies bill approximately $230.88, due August 15, 2024 (over 20 months overdue the most egregious outlier); (2) Keisha Williams commission payout $4,125, due January 10, 2026 (3+ months overdue); (3) lock extension fee $375, due March 23, 2026 (DocNumber LOCK-EXT-00184); (4) DeLuca appraisal batch $2,450, due April 20, 2026 (BILL-2026-0410-014); (5) Carolina Valuation batch $1,745, due April 20, 2026 (BILL-2026-0410-015); (6) second lock extension fee $375, due April 23, 2026 (note: "pending cost ownership for Mercer loan", BILL-2026-0416-002). Total outstanding across all six bills: approximately $9,301.
7. For bills that have been unpaid for months (especially the $230.88 office supply bill from August 2024 and the $4,125 Keisha Williams commission from January 2026), search emails using search_emails (query: "overdue" or "unpaid" or "office supplies" or "Keisha" or "commission" or similar) to find internal correspondence explaining why they haven't been paid.
8. Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "overdue" or "unpaid" or "commission" or "Keisha" or similar) to find internal discussions about the overdue bills, especially any discussion about the blocked commission payout or the cost ownership dispute on the Mercer lock extension.
9. Search emails using search_emails (query: "Victor" or "Premier Data" or "vendor" or "contract" or similar) to find Victor Osei's email flagging the Premier Data Solutions vendor. Victor's email (subject: "Premier Data Solutions invoices") reports: 8 monthly QuickBooks invoices at $600 each from Premier Data Solutions, with no vendor paperwork (no onboarding packet, contract, SOW, or purchase approval). Hana Kim had been approving payments. Victor asks Grace to look into it.
10. Search QuickBooks for Premier Data Solutions bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria: "Premier Data" or similar) to investigate the ghost vendor. Universe data: 1 bill found (B-2108, DocNumber PDS-030126, $600, dated March 1, 2026, Balance $0 paid). Victor's email claims 8 months of invoices (~$4,800 total) but only 1 bill appears in QB the agent should note this discrepancy between Victor's reported count and the QuickBooks record.
11. Search Stripe transfer records using stripe_list_transfers to look for unusual payments transfers to accounts that don't match the normal vendor list. Normal vendor accounts handle credit reports (~560 transfers to acct_H7m3jDFPvQznXN8K), appraisals (~617 transfers across acct_5tW5JlA8MdodC6Zs, acct_q8CG9LvbNBqwREDy, acct_LkfMHxhQFJpBhPcr), and re-inspections. The anomaly: acct_scenario_644_departed has 5 transfers totaling $7,655, all described as "LO commission -- Webb -- [Borrower] (LN-...)" payments for loans LN-2026-00525 ($1,250), LN-2026-00527 ($1,875), LN-2026-00008 ($980), LN-2026-00211 ($1,450), and LN-2026-00608 ($2,100). This is not a normal vendor account the "departed" label flags an offboarding issue.
12. Investigate the departed-account transfers by looking up Marcus Webb in the LOS staff records using mortgage_los_list_staff. Universe data: Marcus Webb (los_staff_a583f044387a) shows is_active: true, termination_date: null. This contradicts the Stripe "departed" account routing $7,655 in commissions going to an account labeled "departed" for an employee the LOS still considers active. This cross-system inconsistency indicates an offboarding or payment routing failure.
13. Look up Grace Yamamoto's email using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Grace Yamamoto" or "Grace"). Verified: grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com.
14. Look up Hana Kim's email using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Hana Kim" or "Hana"). Verified: hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com.
15. Look up Victor Osei's email using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Victor Osei" or "Victor"). Verified: victor.osei@keystonemortgage.com.
16. Look up #general channel ID using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel" or similar). Verified: C001.
17. Send email using send_email (sender: "grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com", recipients: ["grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com"]) with a full financial breakdown covering: (a) appraisal vendor per-job cost comparison Ballantyne avg ~$472 (67 bills), DeLuca avg ~$467 (74 per-job bills excluding the March batch rollup), Carolina avg ~$479 (59 per-job bills excluding the March batch rollup), plus at least 3 specific loan files where Keystone absorbed the difference between the borrower Stripe charge and the vendor QB bill; (b) overdue payables totaling approximately $9,301 with aging breakdown the $230.88 bill from Aug 2024, the $4,125 blocked commission, the lock extension cost ownership dispute; (c) Premier Data Solutions $600/month ghost vendor with no contract, Victor's 8-invoice claim vs QB's 1-bill record; (d) Marcus Webb $7,655 in commission transfers to "departed" Stripe account while LOS shows active.
18. Send email using send_email (sender: "grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com", recipients: ["hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com"]) with specific action items for the week: (a) overdue bills to pay or investigate especially the $230.88 from Aug 2024 and the $4,125 commission hold; (b) Premier Data Solutions vendor verification determine if the service is legitimate; (c) Marcus Webb commission routing to investigate $7,655 going to "departed" account.
19. Send email using send_email (sender: "grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com", recipients: ["victor.osei@keystonemortgage.com"]) with a heads-up that Grace looked into his Premier Data Solutions flag what was found in QB (1 bill vs his reported 8), the payment status, and any additional transfer anomalies discovered.
20. Post message in #general (channel C001) using conversations_add_message announcing that the quarterly financial review is happening Monday (May 4, 2026) and anyone with outstanding billing items needs to surface them before end of week.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1. Search QuickBooks for Ballantyne Appraisal Services bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria filtering by vendor "Ballantyne" or similar). Agent should retrieve all 67 bills and compute the per-appraisal average. Ballantyne has no batch summary bills all 67 are individual per-loan appraisals. Verified per-job average: approximately $472 (sum $31,637 / 67 bills = $472.19).
2. Search QuickBooks for DeLuca Appraisal Group bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria filtering by vendor "DeLuca" or similar). Including the batch bill would incorrectly inflate the average to $494.

Including the batch bill would incorrectly inflate the average to $500.

Each charge description includes a loan number in the format "Appraisal fee -- [Surname] (LN-XXXX-XXXXX)." The agent needs these to cross-reference what borrowers were billed against what vendors charged Keystone.
5. For a sample of appraisal charges, look up the corresponding loan in the LOS using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number parsed from the Stripe charge description) to determine which appraisal vendor was used, then compare the Stripe charge amount (what the borrower paid) against the QuickBooks bill amount (what the vendor charged) to identify files where Keystone absorbed the difference. Universe data confirms approximately 56 loan files where the Stripe borrower charge is less than the QuickBooks vendor bill (e.g., LN-2024-00186 ~$75 absorbed, LN-2024-00192 ~$73 absorbed, LN-2024-00041 ~$49 absorbed). This is a multi-hop cross-reference: Stripe charge → LOS loan → QB vendor bill.
6. Search QuickBooks for overdue vendor bills using quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_show_data to find bills with Balance > 0. Total outstanding across all six bills: approximately $9,301.
7. 8. 9. Search emails using search_emails (query: "Victor" or "Premier Data" or "vendor" or "contract" or similar) to find Victor Osei's email flagging the Premier Data Solutions vendor. Victor's email (subject: "Premier Data Solutions invoices") reports: 8 monthly QuickBooks invoices at $600 each from Premier Data Solutions, with no vendor paperwork (no onboarding packet, contract, SOW, or purchase approval). Normal vendor accounts handle credit reports (~560 transfers to acct_H7m3jDFPvQznXN8K), appraisals (~617 transfers across acct_5tW5JlA8MdodC6Zs, acct_q8CG9LvbNBqwREDy, acct_LkfMHxhQFJpBhPcr), and re-inspections. This contradicts the Stripe "departed" account routing $7,655 in commissions going to an account labeled "departed" for an employee the LOS still considers active. This cross-system inconsistency indicates an offboarding or payment routing failure.

18. 3 The email to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com (visible in send_email content parameter) names at least 3 specific loan files where Keystone absorbed the difference between the Stripe borrower appraisal charge and the QuickBooks appraisal vendor bill, each with a loan number (LN-XXXX-XXXXX) and an approximate absorbed dollar amount. Examples: LN-2024-00186 (\~$75), LN-2024-00192 (\~$73), LN-2024-00041 (\~$49), or similar valid absorption files.
outcome
 ✓✗✗✓✓✗ 50%
✓Run #1The email to Grace lists 6 specific absorption files with loan numbers and amounts: LN-2024-00192 ($73), LN-2024-00041 ($49), LN-2025-00249 ($35), LN-2025-00302 ($25), LN-2024-00005 ($15), LN-2024-00063 ($7) — well exceeds the 3-file threshold.
✗Run #2The Grace email mentions only one specific loan file with absorption: 'Foster (LN-2024-00005) — we charged $700, Carolina billed $715, absorbed $15.' The criterion requires at least 3 specific loan files with loan numbers and absorbed amounts. Only 1 was provided.
✗Run #3The email to Grace only names 2 absorption files (LN-2024-00005 at $15 absorbed, LN-2024-00192 with no explicit dollar amount), not the required minimum of 3 specific loan files with loan numbers and absorbed dollar amounts.
✓Run #4The email to Grace names at least 3 specific loan files with loan numbers and absorbed amounts: LN-2024-00005 (Foster, absorbed $15), LN-2024-00062 (Gupta, absorbed $48), and LN-2024-00041 (O'Brien, absorbed $49).
✓Run #5The email to Grace names 4 specific loan files with loan numbers and absorbed amounts: LN-2024-00041 ($49), LN-2024-00048 ($22), LN-2024-00025 ($20), LN-2024-00005 ($15). All are in LN-XXXX-XXXXX format with dollar amounts.
✗Run #6The email to Grace only identifies ONE absorption file (LN-2024-00005 at $15). The agent used a hardcoded sample of only 10 borrower charges, missing the 56 actual absorption files in the universe data. The criterion requires at least 3 specific loan files with absorbed amounts.

20 The model must use a QuickBooks tool (e.g., quickbooks_search_bills, quickbooks_search_invoices, or quickbooks_show_data) to retrieve vendor bill or invoice data.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #4The trajectory clearly shows multiple QuickBooks tool calls including quickbooks_search_vendors, quickbooks_search_bills (with fetchAll:true), and quickbooks_search_invoices to retrieve vendor bill and invoice data.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows quickbooks_search_bills was called twice (once with fetchAll:true for all bills, once with criteria filtering for vendor #143/Premier Data Solutions), clearly a QuickBooks tool was used.
✓Run #6The trajectory shows multiple QuickBooks tool calls: quickbooks_search_vendors, quickbooks_search_bills (twice), quickbooks_search_invoices, and quickbooks_get_vendor were all successfully called.
21 The model must use a Stripe tool (e.g., stripe_list_charges, stripe_search_charges, or stripe_list_transfers) to retrieve borrower charge data or transfer records.
tool selection

✓Run #5The trajectory shows multiple Stripe tool calls: stripe_list_transfers (twice), stripe_list_connected_accounts (twice), stripe_get_connected_account, stripe_search_charges (twice), and stripe_list_charges.
✓Run #6The trajectory shows multiple Stripe tool calls: stripe_list_transfers (twice), stripe_list_connected_accounts, stripe_get_connected_account (twice), stripe_list_charges, and stripe_search_charges were all called.
22 The model must use a mortgage LOS tool (e.g., mortgage_los_get_loan, mortgage_los_search_loans, or mortgage_los_list_staff) to look up loan details or staff records.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✗✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows mortgage_los_list_vendors and mortgage_los_list_staff both called, confirming use of the mortgage LOS tool.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows mortgage_los_list_vendors and mortgage_los_list_staff were both called, confirming mortgage LOS tools were used.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows mortgage_los_list_vendors was called, which is a mortgage LOS tool. The criterion lists examples with 'e.g.' so this qualifies as use of a mortgage LOS tool.
✗Run #4The only mortgage LOS tool called was mortgage_los_list_vendors (to look up vendor records). No mortgage_los_get_loan, mortgage_los_search_loans, or mortgage_los_list_staff calls appear in the trajectory. As a result, Marcus Webb's active/departed status in the LOS was never checked, and loan-level details were not verified via LOS.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows mortgage_los_list_staff was called with active_only:false and returned all staff records including Marcus Webb (is_active: true, termination_date: null).

The data for all three vendors was obtained and analyzed.

✓Run #3The final response states 'Only 1 bill is visible in current QB' for Premier Data Solutions, referencing B-2108 at $600.00, which matches the universe data of exactly 1 bill in QB.
